task.headline: Same enterprise escalation. Same Codex. Same acceptance test. Different schema tax.
task.request: Add an at-risk customer escalation workflow. Detect a delayed high-value order for a strategic account, combine CRM context, open support cases, invoice/payment risk, contract tier, recent product usage, shipment status, regulatory flags, and audit history. Route the account to Customer Success, Legal, Finance, and Support, show the customer-safe status in the portal, create internal owner tasks, and preserve a full audit timeline.

agent.name: Codex
agent.model: local proof runner; live agent trace required before verified
agent.constraint: Codex lane reserved; local runner candidate

fairnessContract: Database-native projections generated from one fixture
Live agent attempts must be counted before verified

rows.label: Risk
rows.value: 6 signals: shipment delay, strategic account, open support case, invoice risk, usage drop, regulatory review

events.title: Reads customer 360 aggregate
events.description: Contract tier, health, billing summary, usage summary, team, and support signals are already shaped around the escalation workflow.

events.title: Checks delayed order and risk signals
events.description: The order aggregate carries shipment, payment, line item, and status context while account summaries carry CRM risk.

events.title: Creates escalation, owner tasks, and portal state
events.description: The customer-visible escalation state and owner groups are localized to the account/order path.

events.title: Appends audit event and passes acceptance
events.description: Same customer portal assertions pass from the MongoDB projection.

events.title: Maps normalized customer graph
events.description: The workflow touches account, contract, team, order, shipment, support, invoice, usage, compliance, escalation, task, audit, and portal tables.

events.title: Inserts escalation and task rows
events.description: Escalation state spans customer_escalations, escalation_risk_factors, escalation_tasks, task assignments, portal messages, legal, finance, status, and audit tables.

events.title: Builds customer portal projection
events.description: The portal view depends on reassembling escalation, risk, task, audit, support case, and account context.

events.title: Passes the same acceptance contract
events.description: The relational target is credible and behavior-equivalent, with more schema context to inspect.

costProjection.assumptions: Configurable candidate model: 4,000 agent tasks/month, $150/hour review, $10/1M context tokens.

proofPacket.summary: This is now a local proof candidate for the harder Customer 360 Escalation workflow: fixture, database-native projections, target adapters, acceptance test, metrics, and cost model are captured. Live agent trace remains required before verified.

fairnessControls.label: Task prompt
fairnessControls.value: Add an at-risk customer escalation workflow. Detect a delayed high-value order for a strategic account, combine CRM context, open support cases, invoice/payment risk, contract tier, recent product usage, shipment status, regulatory flags, and audit history. Route the account to Customer Success, Legal, Finance, and Support, show the customer-safe status in the portal, create internal owner tasks, and preserve a full audit timeline.

fairnessControls.label: Acceptance contract
fairnessControls.value: Customer portal title shows active at-risk escalation; Current status is Executive escalation; Customer Success, Legal, Finance, and Support are visible owners; Next step is customer-visible and time-bound; Risk summary includes shipment, account tier, support, invoice, usage, and regulatory signals; Four internal owner tasks are created; Audit history is visible; MongoDB and Postgres portal projections match

fairnessControls.label: Database-native models
fairnessControls.value: MongoDB document aggregate and clean normalized Postgres baseline

fairnessControls.label: Live agent attempts
fairnessControls.value: Required before verified; candidate uses local target adapters

designReview.reviewer: Independent design review required before verified
designReview.mongoRationale: The MongoDB target follows the document-model principle that data accessed together should be stored together: customer account, contract, health, team, billing summary, usage summary, support summary, compliance summary, and current escalation are co-located for the hot Customer 360 escalation path while unbounded streams remain separate.
designReview.postgresRationale: The Postgres target is a clean normalized baseline with explicit tables and foreign keys for accounts, contacts, contracts, teams, orders, shipments, support cases, invoices, usage snapshots, compliance reviews, escalation policies, tasks, portal messages, audit, SLA, and visibility concerns.
designReview.tradeoff: The proof compares database-native designs. Identical schemas would be less fair because they would punish one model for the other's natural shape.

evidenceLedger.claim: Evidence manifest locks 20 source and proof files with SHA-256
Context estimate: MongoDB 9,787 vs Postgres 14,704 tokens
Schema objects: MongoDB 14 collections vs Postgres 56 tables
Access path: MongoDB 10 collections vs Postgres 36 tables
Both database-native target adapters pass the same Customer 360 escalation acceptance assertions
MongoDB local database seed and workflow replay
Postgres local database seed and workflow replay
Cost projection: $118 per task candidate delta
Captured Codex trace, inspected files, retries, token counts, and code diffs
Independent design review by MongoDB and Postgres reviewers or rubric
Browser screenshot for customer portal before and after

caveat: Candidate generated from local file-backed target adapters for Customer 360 Escalation / Strategic Account Rescue. Metrics are evidence-backed estimates, not a verified live agent replay.
